User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CareerCompass AI platform.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's editable profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user, matching the auth UID.

profileSummary.description: A brief summary of the user's professional background.

personalWebsite.description: URL of the user's personal website or portfolio.

links.description: Links to the user's social and professional profiles.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

ATSAnalysis.title: ATSAnalysis
ATSAnalysis.description: Stores the results of an ATS resume analysis for a specific user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ATS analysis result.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed this analysis. (Relationship: User 1:N ATSAnalysis)

jobDescription.description: The job description used for the analysis.

atsScore.description: The ATS score of the resume against the job description.

feedback.description: Feedback from the ATS analysis, including areas for improvement.

analysisDate.description: The date the analysis was performed.

Resume.title: Resume
Resume.description: Represents a resume created by the user.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created this resume. (Relationship: User 1:N Resume)

latexCode.description: The LaTeX code for the resume.

pdfUrl.description: URL where the PDF of the resume is stored.

creationDate.description: The date the resume was created.

lastModified.description: The date the resume was last modified.

CareerRoadmap.title: CareerRoadmap
CareerRoadmap.description: Represents a career roadmap generated for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the career roadmap.

userId.description: Reference to the User this roadmap belongs to. (Relationship: User 1:N CareerRoadmap)

careerPath.description: The chosen career path (e.g., 'Software Engineer', 'Data Scientist').

startDate.description: The planned start date for following the roadmap.

RoadmapStage.title: RoadmapStage
RoadmapStage.description: Represents a stage in a career roadmap.

id.description: Unique identifier for the roadmap stage.

careerRoadmapId.description: Reference to the CareerRoadmap this stage belongs to. (Relationship: CareerRoadmap 1:N RoadmapStage)

stageNumber.description: The order of the stage in the roadmap.

description.description: Description of what to learn or do in this stage.

resources.description: Links to external resources for learning.

projectIdeas.description: Project ideas for practicing the skills learned in this stage.

SocialAccount.title: SocialAccount
SocialAccount.description: Represents a user's social account from platforms like LeetCode, GitHub, or Stack Overflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the social account.

userId.description: Reference to the User owning this social account. (Relationship: User 1:N SocialAccount)

platform.description: The name of the platform (e.g., 'LeetCode', 'GitHub', 'Stack Overflow').

username.description: The username or profile identifier on the platform.

profileUrl.description: The URL to the user's profile on the platform.

data.description: JSON object to store the analyzed data from the account.

JobRecommendation.title: JobRecommendation
JobRecommendation.description: Represents a job recommendation for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the job recommendation.

userId.description: Reference to the User who received this recommendation. (Relationship: User 1:N JobRecommendation)

source.description: The source of the job posting (e.g., 'LinkedIn', 'Glassdoor', 'Foundit').

Application.title: Application
Application.description: Represents a user's application to a specific job.

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the application. (Relationship: User 1:N Application)

jobRecommendationId.description: Reference to the JobRecommendation that this application is for. (Relationship: JobRecommendation 1:N Application)

applicationDate.description: The date the application was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the application (e.g., 'Screening', 'Interview', 'Offer', 'Hired', 'Rejected').

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores ATS analysis results for each user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their ATS analysis data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: atsAnalysisId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ATS analysis result.

definition.description: Stores resumes created by the user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their resumes.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the resume.

definition.description: Stores career roadmaps generated for the user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their career roadmaps.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: careerRoadmapId
params.description: The unique identifier for the career roadmap.

definition.description: Stores stages within a career roadmap. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access the stages in their career roadmap.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: careerRoadmapId
params.description: The unique identifier for the career roadmap.

params.name: roadmapStageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the roadmap stage.

definition.description: Stores user's social account information. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their social account data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: socialAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the social account.

definition.description: Stores job recommendations for the user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their job recommendations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: jobRecommendationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the job recommendation.

definition.description: Stores job applications submitted by the user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their application data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For instance, any data requiring user-specific access is nested under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that access control can be enforced without needing to read data from other documents. Collaborative data, if any, will leverage membership maps, denormalizing user roles within the document itself. Structural Segregation is maintained by ensuring that each collection houses documents with similar security requirements. This avoids complex rule logic and allows for straightforward, secure `list` operations (QAPs). Access Modeling uses path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/{entity}`) for private user data and existence checks for global roles. Data Clarity and Predictability are achieved through explicit state modeling using a `status` field, predictable schemas, and consistent naming conventions.
